text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Talk is cheap. Show me the code.
author: Linus Torvalds

text: Stay hungry, stay foolish.
author: Steve Jobs

text: First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.
author: John Johnson

text: Knowledge is power.
author: Francis Bacon

text: Quality is not an act, it is a habit.
author: Aristotle

text: Hardware is the part of a computer that you can kick.
author: Jeff Pesis

text: Software is a gas; it expands to fill its container.
author: Nathan Myhrvold

text: The most disastrous thing that you can ever learn is your first programming language.
author: Alan Kay

text: Truth can only be found in one place: the code.
author: Robert C. Martin

text: Walking on water and developing software from a specification are easy if both are frozen.
author: Edward V. Berard

text: Your passion is waiting for your courage to catch up.
author: Isabelle Lafleche

text: Magic is believing in yourself. If you can do that, you can make anything happen.
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

text: If you are the smartest person in the room, you are in the wrong room.
author: Lorne Michaels

text: Hard days are the best days because that's when champions are made.
author: Gabby Douglas

text: Don't stop when you're tired. Stop when you're done.
author: David Goggins

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Aristotle

text: Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated.
author: Confucius

text: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: It is not death that a man should fear, but he should fear never beginning to live.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Small opportunities are often the beginning of great enterprises.
author: Demosthenes
